text.title: Log Collection with Filebeat and Elastic Beats Core Rules
text.summary: 5 rules grounded in Filebeat docs + K8s production patterns.

rule.statement: On K8s, Filebeat MUST be deployed as a DaemonSet (one pod per node). Sidecar-per-pod patterns are forbidden.
rule.rationale: DaemonSet means O(nodes) pods, not O(containers). Sidecar pattern multiplies log shipper count by container count → wasted CPU + memory.

rule.statement: Every input MUST set `fields_under_root: true`. Nested `fields.service` paths in queries are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Nested fields break flat dashboards + alerting rules that assume top-level field names. Default behaviour is nesting — must be explicitly set.

rule.statement: Multiline-capable sources (Java / Python tracebacks) MUST configure `multiline.pattern` + `multiline.negate` + `multiline.match`. Single-line-per-event default is forbidden when source produces multiline events.
rule.rationale: Without multiline, every stack-trace line becomes its own document — breaks search + makes alerting unusable.

rule.statement: K8s deployments MUST enable Kubernetes autodiscover provider (or hints-based). Static input lists are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Static inputs require config updates on every deploy. Autodiscover picks up new pods automatically + cleans up on terminate.

rule.statement: Output target (Elasticsearch / Logstash) MUST be pinned to a versioned endpoint with TLS + auth. Plain HTTP outputs are forbidden in prod.
rule.rationale: Plain HTTP leaks log content. Unauth output lets any host on the network inject logs.

rule.statement: If the Applies If preconditions in AGENTS.md do not all hold, OR any Skip If trigger fires, the agent MUST skip this methodology and pick a different one — applying the rules here on a mismatched input produces wrong output.
rule.rationale: Methodology selection errors cascade. The cheapest place to abort is before any rule fires.
